metadata.title: The Runaway Hypothesis (R1)
metadata.description: The simple version of the winner-takes-all story: a better model produces better code, which makes the lab itself more productive, which produces an even better next model.
metadata.author: Tracking AI Course

nodes.label: Model Capability
nodes.description: Aggregate quality of the lab's frontier model — reasoning, code generation, agentic task completion.

nodes.label: Code Generation Quality
nodes.description: How reliably the model produces correct, maintainable code for real engineering tasks.

nodes.label: R&D Productivity
nodes.description: How fast the AI lab itself can ship experiments, training infrastructure, and next-gen models.

edges.description: Better code generation accelerates the lab's own engineering work.
Faster R&D produces stronger next-generation models.

delay.description: Training cycles introduce a real delay between productivity gains and capability gains.

loops.label: R1: Recursive Self-Improvement
loops.description: The core 'winner-takes-all' hypothesis: better models build better models.
loops.behavior_pattern: Compounding capability growth, bounded only by external constraints.
